macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: S&P 500 Posts Fifth Consecutive Weekly Loss Amid Middle East Tensions and Oil Price Surge
items.summary: The S&P 500 fell 2% over the past week, marking its first five-week losing streak since 2022, as Middle East conflict concerns and rising oil prices to $112/barrel weigh on markets. The index is pacing for its worst month in a year.

items.title: U.S. Treasury Yields Rise to 4.43% as Inflation Concerns Mount
items.summary: U.S. 10-year Treasury yields climbed to 4.43% amid elevated oil prices and questions about whether central banks can keep pace with inflation. The key policy question has shifted from rate cuts to whether policy rates will keep up with rising inflation.

items.title: Fed Official Speech and Economic Data on Tap This Week
items.summary: Fed Waller is scheduled to speak, while key labor market data across the U.S., euro area, and Japan will be released this week to assess whether recent economic softness signals broader cooling or continued resilience.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Drops Below $78K Amid Heavy Liquidations and Market Pullback
items.summary: Bitcoin fell to around $77,300 after failing to hold above $80,000, with over $125 million in BTC liquidations and total crypto liquidations exceeding $330-600 million in the past 24 hours. Ethereum hit an intraday low of $2,267, while XRP and Dogecoin also retreated amid surging trading volumes and bearish sentiment.

items.title: Over 110,000 Crypto Traders Liquidated in 24 Hours Due to Volatility
items.summary: A record 110,878 traders were liquidated across major exchanges, with $400-600 million in positions wiped out from sharp swings in Bitcoin and Ethereum prices. This highlights the risks of leveraged trading as long positions dominated the losses.

items.title: Crypto Market Cap at $2.64T as Major Coins Decline Before Fed Decision
items.summary: The global crypto market capitalization reached $2.64 trillion after a brief 1.93% spike, but Bitcoin, Ethereum (-3.76%), XRP (-3.05%), and others retreated amid anticipation of the Federal Reserve's policy decision on April 28-29. Traders are reducing leverage ahead of potential uncertainty.

items.title: Bitcoin Spikes to $79,480 Then Reverses on Oil Surge and Failed $80K Break
items.summary: Bitcoin briefly hit $79,480 before dropping 2% to $77,800 in an hour, influenced by rising oil prices and heavy selling pressure near resistance levels. No specific news triggered the move, but derivatives activity amplified the decline.

items.title: Crypto Sentiment Shifts to Fear as Trump Discusses Iran Proposal
items.summary: Leading cryptocurrencies retreated while stocks hit records, with the Crypto Fear & Greed Index flipping to 'Fear' amid Trump's discussion of an Iranian war-ending proposal and climbing oil prices. Over $330 million in long positions were liquidated.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Wall Street Split on Quantum Computing Bet
items.summary: Experts highlight quantum computing's potential to reshape drug discovery, machine learning, and financial risk modeling, though Wall Street remains divided on investment prospects.

items.title: President Lee meets 'Father of AlphaGo' Hassabis
items.summary: South Korean President Lee meets Demis Hassabis, CEO of Google DeepMind, to discuss AI collaboration, research partnerships, and talent development, while addressing AI safety concerns.

items.title: Generative Video Technology Advances Demonstrated
items.summary: Fan-made Transformers scene showcases significant progress in generative video technology, with realistic explosions, motion, and scale comparable to professional productions.

items.title: OpenAI and Microsoft Partnership Changes
items.summary: OpenAI ends exclusive partnership with Microsoft, signaling shifts in major AI industry collaborations and market dynamics.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Alien Isolation 2 Announced
items.summary: Alien Isolation 2 has been officially announced. The announcement was discussed alongside news about Xbox rebranding and price reductions.

items.title: The Division 2 Servers Down for Prototype Gear Removal and Drop Increases
items.summary: The Division 2 servers are going down to remove prototype gear and increase drop rates. This maintenance was announced as breaking news.

items.title: Roblox Sues Bad Games, Implements Enhanced Protection and Chat Updates
items.summary: Roblox has taken legal action against problematic games and introduced new safety features including enhanced protection systems and chat updates. The platform is also phasing out 'brainrot' content.

items.title: Assassin's Creed Hexe Loses 50 Developers, Shifts Direction Toward 2027 Release
items.summary: Assassin's Creed Hexe has experienced significant staff changes with 50 developers departing, leading to a change in direction and a planned 2027 release date.

items.title: Stanley Cup Playoffs: Flyers vs. Penguins Game 5, Oilers Trail Ducks 3-1
items.summary: In NHL Stanley Cup Playoff action, the Edmonton Oilers trail the Anaheim Ducks 3-1 in their series, with Game 5 upcoming. The Philadelphia Flyers and Pittsburgh Penguins are also in playoff competition.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Quantum Motion Deploys First Full-Stack Silicon Quantum Computer
items.summary: Quantum Motion has achieved a milestone by deploying the first full-stack quantum computer using standard silicon chip fabrication processes, potentially accelerating scalable quantum systems.

items.title: Google and Qualcomm Push Android PCs into Mainstream
items.summary: Google and Qualcomm are advancing Android PCs to challenge Microsoft and Apple, aiming to scale the mobile OS to desktops and reshape personal computing.

items.title: X Launches Dedicated XChat Messaging App for iOS
items.summary: X has introduced XChat, a new dedicated messaging app for iOS users.

items.title: Oracle Lays Off Over 20,000 Employees Worldwide
items.summary: Oracle has initiated layoffs affecting more than 20,000 employees globally amid restructuring efforts.

items.title: SpaceX Selected as Contractor for US Project
items.summary: SpaceX has been chosen as one of the contractors for a US government initiative.

items.title: Bitcoin Surges Past $70,000 Barrier
items.summary: Bitcoin has broken through $70,000 with a 4% surge in 24 hours, pushing its market value beyond $1.4 trillion.
